data.description:
## Summary

`rebar criteria eval <project.criterion>` (and the underlying
`calibrate_criterion`) cannot run the LIVE calibration of any project criterion
whose logical id contains the `project.` namespace dot, because the physical
prompt-id â†’ criterion-id round-trip is lossy.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Activate a project criterion via `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` (e.g.
   `project.portability`, `exec: 1-TURN`) with a rubric prompt and an eval fixture
   at `.rebar/evals/plan-review-project-portability.eval.yaml`.
2. Run `rebar criteria eval project.portability --runs 1 --output json`.

Expected: the criterion runs live over the dataset and prints calibration metrics.

Actual: exits non-zero with
`ValueError: no eval solver for prompt 'plan-review-project-portability'`
from `eval_solver.run_case`.

## Root Cause

`rebar.llm.evals.eval.calibrate_criterion` computes
`prompt_id = criterion_prompt_id("project.portability")` = `plan-review-project-portability`
(the forward map replaces `.`â†’`-`) to load the eval fixture (correct). But the default
`_criterion_solver.solve(prompt_id, case)` passes that DASH prompt-id to
`eval_solver.run_case`, which resolves the criterion via `_criterion_id(prompt_id)`:
it strips the `plan-review-` prefix to `project-portability` (dash) and checks
`cid in registry.by_id(repo_root)`. The registry is keyed by the LOGICAL id
`project.portability` (dot), so the dash form is never found â†’ `_criterion_id` returns
None â†’ `run_case` raises `no eval solver`. Built-in ids (no dot) round-trip fine; only
namespaced `project.<name>` criteria break. Confirmed:
`_criterion_id("plan-review-project-portability", root)` â†’ None, while
`_criterion_id("project.portability", root)` â†’ "project.portability".

## Proposed Fix (one of)

- `_criterion_solver` / `calibrate_criterion`: resolve the criterion in `run_case` from
  the ORIGINAL logical `criterion_id` (dot), using the dash `prompt_id` only for
  `load_eval_spec`; or
- `eval_solver._criterion_id`: after stripping `plan-review-`, also try mapping a leading
  `project-<name>` back to `project.<name>` (the namespaced project-criterion inverse),
  since `<name>` has no dots.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar criteria eval <project.criterion>` resolves and runs the project criterion's
      Pass-1 finder over its dataset (no `no eval solver` error), for a criterion whose
      logical id contains the `project.` namespace dot.
- [ ] A regression test drives `calibrate_criterion` (or `eval_solver.run_case`) for an
      activated `project.<name>` criterion with an injected/fake runner and asserts the
      criterion resolves (fires/does-not-fire per the fake), proving the physicalâ†’logical
      round-trip. File: `tests/unit/test_criteria_eval.py` (or a sibling).
- [ ] Built-in criterion calibration (e.g. `F1`) is unchanged (no regression).
data.title: Live criteria-eval cannot resolve namespaced project criteria (dash/dot round-trip)
